title: Causal Change-Point Features Within Each Stint
summary: The current pipeline uses level features like cumulative degradation and lap-time delta, but not explicit detection of a pace-cliff or regime switch.
rationale: The current pipeline uses level features like cumulative degradation and lap-time delta, but not explicit detection of a pace-cliff or regime switch. Recent F1 tyre modeling uses latent state evolution with pit-stop resets, and online change-point methods are designed to flag abrupt baseline shifts in streaming time series.
implementation_hint: Reconstruct causal sequences by `Year/Race/Driver/LapNumber`. Within each stint, compute online scores such as BOCPD change probability, current run length, and CUSUM-style deviations on `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, and residualized lap time. Feed only current and past information, never centered windows.
expected_effect: Better capture of sudden ‘box now’ states caused by tyre cliffs, traffic-clean-air transitions, or weather/track-state shifts that raw cumulative features smooth away.
risk: If row ordering or race reconstruction is imperfect, these features can leak or become noisy. They also need careful normalization across compounds and wet/dry laps.
